userMessage:
My signal has intermittent glitches around 50ns wide on CH2. How should I set up the trigger to catch them reliably?
Make sure to add two reliability steps if missing:

ACQuire:STOPAfter SEQuence
ACQuire:STATE RUN

warnings: Detected over-concatenated SCPI command strings; split into grouped steps (max 3 per step).

response.text:
I built a single-sequence CH2 pulse-width trigger flow for sub-50 ns glitches and added the two acquisition reliability steps.

actionsJson.summary: Create a new pyvisa scope flow that configures an A pulse-width trigger on CH2 for pulses shorter than 50 ns, reads back the key trigger settings, enables single-sequence acquisition, and arms the scope.
findings: Polarity for the glitch was not specified, so the flow leaves the existing pulse-width polarity unchanged.
suggestedFixes: If the glitches are known to be only positive or only negative, add the matching TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:POLarity command for better selectivity.
Auto-repaired missing/duplicate step ids in replace_flow actions for apply compatibility.
Long semicolon command chains were split and grouped for readability (max 3 commands per step).
Filled connect/disconnect instrumentIds with workspace alias "MSO6B" for direct chat apply compatibility.

flow.name: Catch CH2 50 ns Glitches
flow.description: Configure a CH2 pulse-width trigger for glitches shorter than 50 ns, verify the setup, enable single-sequence capture, and arm the scope.
